methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Naïfo Technologies is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company founded by Gaëtan Séverac that has raised €54.7M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of nine same-sector, same- or adjacent-stage companies, comprising eight private firms and one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ). Named peers include 1X Technologies ($10.0B Series B), Exotec ($2.0B Series D), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B Series B), and Hadrian ($1.6B Series C).

The peer-median method is the sole applicable triangulation approach given available data. The nine-company peer cohort exhibits a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range spanning $300M (low) to $10.0B (high). This median directly anchors the valuation estimate.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.4B (low) to $1.8B (high), centered at $1.6B. The median aligns with Stage C comparables and reflects observed pricing across a cohort with material heterogeneity—the $10.0B ceiling reflects outlier performance (1X Technologies at Series B), while the $300M floor indicates variability in downstream stage outcomes. Key uncertainties include capital efficiency relative to peer cohort and the applicability of the single public comparable to private market pricing. Deeper stage-adjusted analysis would require additional operational metrics not present in current data.
